message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Stage register test, commit, and push">
Kent, I see the .sqlx files are staged now — good. But there's also a modified `tests/api/register.rs` that's unstaged, and the branch is STILL 2 commits ahead of origin (not pushed). Please:

1. Stage `tests/api/register.rs` if it's green
2. Commit
3. `git push`

We need to stop accumulating local-only commits. This is exactly the problem the new rules address.
</teammate-message>

input.command: git fetch origin 2>&1 && echo "=== AHEAD ===" && git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD 2>&1 && echo "=== STATUS ===" && git status 2>&1
input.description: Fresh fetch and full state check

content.text: After a fresh `git fetch`: zero commits ahead of origin, working tree clean, nothing to commit. Everything is already pushed.

input.content:
Kent Beck: just ran `git fetch origin` again. The result is definitive:

```
$ git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
(empty - zero commits ahead)

$ git status
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
nothing to commit, working tree clean
```

There is nothing to stage, nothing to commit, nothing to push. Local and origin are identical at `e2cda97`. The `.sqlx/` files, `tests/api/register.rs`, and all other files are already committed and pushed.

I believe there's a timing lag between my pushes and when your view of the repo updates. If you run `git fetch origin && git log --oneline -3 origin/main` from your side, you should see `e2cda97` at the top.
input.summary: Confirmed again: zero ahead, clean tree, everything pushed

routing.content:
Kent Beck: just ran `git fetch origin` again. The result is definitive:

```
$ git log --oneline origin/main..HEAD
(empty - zero commits ahead)

$ git status
On branch main
Your branch is up to date with 'origin/main'.
nothing to commit, working tree clean
```

There is nothing to stage, nothing to commit, nothing to push. Local and origin are identical at `e2cda97`. The `.sqlx/` files, `tests/api/register.rs`, and all other files are already committed and pushed.

I believe there's a timing lag between my pushes and when your view of the repo updates. If you run `git fetch origin && git log --oneline -3 origin/main` from your side, you should see `e2cda97` at the top.

content.text: Everything is committed and pushed. The team lead's messages appear to be arriving with a delay relative to the actual git state. I've confirmed the state three times now with fresh fetches. Standing by.
